title: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Pregnant Individuals - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Pregnant Individuals 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Domestic Violence Issues Homeless Families Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Human Trafficking Issues Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO COORDINATED ENTRY FOR FAMILIES MULTNOMAH COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Multnomah County Coordinated Entry for Families is a partnership of different agencies through Multnomah County’s Homeless Service Department (HSD) and consists of both the Supportive Housing & Family Shelter program. 211info completes eligibility and program enrollment via a Multnomah Services and Screening Tool (MSST) assessment. If families are eligible, partnering agencies within the community connect with families and offer services related to shelter/housing access. Meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee access to services. Households are prioritized for assistance based on various factors, including availability of resources. This is not an immediate service and the wait time can be a few months to a year. It is encouraged that households continue seeking other resources and support. This program CANNOT assist financially with evictions to maintain current housing. It may be able to help with future housing if the household becomes homeless. Eligibility: Families with children in their custody under the age of 18 (or plans for reunification) - OR - a third trimester pregnancy (28 weeks/7 months) with connections to Multnomah County (i.e., was displaced, has kids in school, job, service providers in Multnomah County, etc.) AND meet one of the following criteria: -In emergency shelter, transitional housing, motel paid for by an agency or a place not meant for habitation (i.e. car, streets, tent, abandoned building, etc.) -Doubled up (with family/friends and not on the lease, unsafely doubled up/living situation is over safe capacity) and at risk of becoming literally homeless within 14 days -Housed, including self pay at a motel, and will become literally homeless within 14 days or has received a court ordered eviction notice OR has a self-identified EHV voucher set to expire in Fall of 2026 -In an institution (i.e. jail, hospital, substance use or mental health treatment facility, etc.) and at risk of exiting to homelessness within 14 days -Fleeing or attempting to flee domestic violence and lacks the resources or support networks to obtain other safe, permanent housing Hours: Added to callback List for Assessment: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Assessment: 7 days per week 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
